name: Housing & construction
description: Starts, permits, prices, sales, and construction spending for homebuilders, REITs, and building products.

series.note: Isolates single-family starts, the highest-margin and most volume-relevant segment for production homebuilders versus multifamily apartment supply.
Tracks multifamily/apartment construction, a direct demand signal for apartment REIT supply pipelines and a leading indicator of future rent growth.
Building permits lead starts by 1-2 months, giving the earliest read on the future construction pipeline and builder confidence in demand.
New home sales are the direct top-line demand metric for homebuilders and the cleanest gauge of new-construction absorption.
Months' supply of new homes signals inventory/demand balance and pricing power; rising supply pressures builder gross margins and incentives.
Existing sales are ~85% of the market and drive demand for remodeling, real estate brokerage, title, and home-improvement retail.
Median new-home price tracks builder pricing power and revenue per unit, a core driver of homebuilder margins and average selling price.
The benchmark repeat-sales home price index; rising prices support homeowner equity, refi/HELOC volume, and mortgage credit quality.
FHFA's broad purchase/refi price index used for LTV and collateral valuation; complements Case-Shiller for mortgage credit-risk analysis.
Dollar value of residential construction put in place, a direct revenue proxy for builders, building products, and materials demand.
Commercial/industrial construction spending differentiates nonresidential-exposed contractors, materials, and industrial REIT development.
Aggregate construction put-in-place captures total end-market demand for cement, aggregates, machinery, and engineering & construction firms.
Rental vacancy is the supply/demand barometer for apartment REIT occupancy and pricing power; lower vacancy supports rent growth.
Homeowner vacancy gauges excess for-sale inventory and shadow supply, an early signal of price softening and absorption stress.
Residential mortgage delinquencies are the core housing credit-quality metric driving bank loss provisions and mortgage-insurer claims.
CRE delinquencies flag stress in office/retail lending, critical for regional banks and commercial mortgage REIT credit analysis.
